Tutorial - GMS2 Top Down Shooter

If you want to see tutorial in a separate web browser window click here.

Getting Started

This tutorial is a more advanced tutorial than Space Rocks or First Look. I have left the scripting in images so you can't cut and paste the text. When learning to script I think it is very important to type it in yourself and get used to making mistakes and develop an understanding of what you are typing does to the game engine. For that reason I have eliminated the ability to cut and paste scripts.

We will be making a vastly simplified version of the original 1942 Arcade Game. If you are not familiar with the game take a look at this playthrough of the original video game.

We need to make sure we see the files that are incluced with this tutorial. There are two folders called TutorialResources and two folders Sprites and Sounds. If you don't see them in the Resources tab then you can find the two folders here. Inside are all the files we need for the game. Please note where this is located as you will need to access them on the next page.

Normally you would start a game from scratch. Since this tutorial creates a game for you. I will show you the process (you don't need to do this for this tutorial) if you wanted to start a GML game from scratch. You would select New then GameMaker Language and then put it in a folder and give the project a name:

On the next page we will start by getting the plane started.
